_patch_meta.description: C17 Dimension Review — Part 3: Reg 103 (love, 66 groups) + Session B/D pointers for Reg 23, 34, 62, 64, 99, 103

set.notes: Friendship as the inner-relational bond grounded in knowledge and trust. Automated label Agency/Power (KEYWORD_STRONG) misassigned — the keyword match triggered on power in verse context. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
Both divine and human subjects span the 7-verse corpus. Automated label Relational Disposition confirmed.
Intense inner emotional states expressed through the kiss — love, grief, contrition, joyful reunion. Automated label confirmed.
Disordered inner orientation of affection toward the world — relational disposition toward wrong object. UNCLASSIFIED label corrected to Relational Disposition.
Loyal friendship as inner character quality — faithfulness that holds even when costly. Automated label Moral Character confirmed.
The beloved as the object of consuming inner longing and delight. Automated label confirmed.

Sister as relational identity defined by spiritual orientation and moral care. Automated label Volition corrected — the result (relational bond) is the focus, not the act of choosing. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
The brotherhood as the object of love and context of shared suffering. Automated label confirmed.
Concealed deception masquerading as fellowship — a stable inner moral character disposition. UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Moral Character.
Divine compassion as defining relational disposition. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence corrected — neither anchor shows mirroring structure. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.
The neighbour as the moral category defining inner obligations. Automated label confirmed.
Loyalty shown or withheld within the companion bond. Automated label confirmed.

Trusted loyalty as the ground of courageous action. Automated label Dependence/Creatureliness is clearly misassigned — this is relational loyalty between humans, not creaturely dependence before God. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
Companion in communal grief — shared inner lamentation. Automated label confirmed.
The peacemaker as a stable inner character orientation. UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Moral Character.
Hatred as a relational disposition — directed toward another. UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Relational Disposition.
Identity formation through conformity to the firstborn Son — inner-being process of being shaped into Christs image. Automated label Volition corrected to Transformation.
Inner contempt for sacred inheritance — moral character failure of despising the holy. Automated label confirmed.

The love-relationship in its full range spanning divine and human subjects. Automated label Emotion-Positive corrected — dod names the relational bond (beloved), not merely an inner feeling. Correcting to Relational Disposition.

Gods deepest attachment to the beloved — election-love expressed as gift and grief. Automated label Emotion-Positive misses the relational-disposition quality — ya.did names a relational bond (the beloved), not an inner feeling. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.

Gods most intimate attachment — the beloved of my soul. Automated label Emotion-Negative corrected — the grief is the consequence; the characteristic is the relational bond. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.

Gods steadfast love as his defining relational disposition. Automated label Moral Character corrected — chesed is directional/relational, always toward someone. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.
Human chesed as inner character quality of covenantal faithfulness. Automated label confirmed.
Absence/withdrawal of chesed as inner moral failure; chesed as the priority God requires. Automated label confirmed.
Gods everlasting love as covenantal inner disposition. Automated label confirmed.

The intense inner experience of mutual love — longing, delight, exclusive attachment in its most burning form. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected — the emotional intensity and felt quality dominate here over the relational orientation. Correcting to Emotion — Positive.
Love as moral loyalty — inner character orientation of faithfulness. Automated label confirmed.
Inner experience of love-delight in its ordered and disordered forms. Automated label confirmed.
The godly person defined by covenantal faithfulness — stable inner character. Automated label Volition corrected to Moral Character.

The inner act of assessing, judging, or recognising something as good — whether divine approval or personal evaluative recognition. Automated label Emotion-Positive corrected — the evaluative/cognitive act is primary across both anchors. Correcting to Cognition. NONE — both divine and human subjects anchored.

Gods deep inner movement of compassion — the ra.cha.mim as divine relational disposition. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence corrected — description and both anchors primarily name Gods own disposition rather than a structural mirroring. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.
The inner experience of love in its idealising, delighting form. Automated label confirmed.

Inner recognition of fittingness and beauty — the cognitive-aesthetic act of discerning what is na.eh. Automated label Emotion-Positive corrected — na.eh names what is recognised as fitting or beautiful (a cognitive act of discernment). Correcting to Cognition.
The inner disposition of lust — corrupted desire orienting the will toward disordered objects. Automated label confirmed.
Inner experience of pleasantness and delight. Automated label confirmed.
Hatred as inner moral disposition — righteous and disordered forms. Automated label (KEYWORD_STRONG) confirmed. NONE — 134-verse corpus spans both divine and human subjects.

Gods visceral inner movement of compassion. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence corrected — both anchors show Gods own experience of compassion toward Israel, not a mirroring structure. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.

Inner orientation of love directed toward God. Automated label Emotion-Positive corrected — filotheos names a directional relational orientation (toward God), not a positive feeling. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
Tender family-like inner affection toward fellow believers. Automated label (ROOT_INFERRED) confirmed.

The Fathers love for the Son as the paradigmatic inner-love relationship — constitutively divine and the pattern for all other love relationships. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected — the divine beloved relationship is the source from which the believers agapetos status flows (555-002), creating a correspondence structure. Correcting to Divine-Human Correspondence, GOD.
The believer as the cherished object of Gods love — the relational identity of belovedness. Automated label confirmed.

Gods benevolent inner disposition toward humanity. Automated label Divine-Human Correspondence corrected — unilateral divine disposition, not a correspondence. Correcting to Relational Disposition, GOD.
Brotherly love as the inner-relational disposition binding the community. Automated label confirmed.
Love of children as a cultivated inner relational orientation. Automated label confirmed.
The contentious inner inclination — stable character disposition toward argument. Automated label Relational Disposition (ROOT_INFERRED) corrected to Moral Character.
Love of husband as cultivated inner relational orientation. Automated label Transformation (KEYWORD_STRONG) is clearly misassigned. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
Gods love as the inner source and ground of all love. Automated label confirmed.

Love as the defining inner virtue — stable character quality produced by the Spirit. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected — 1 Cor 13 presents love as a character portrait, not a relational act. Correcting to Moral Character.
Mutual love as the communal inner orientation. Automated label confirmed.
Love of dispute as inner character disposition of rivalry. Automated label Relational Disposition (KEYWORD_STRONG) corrected to Moral Character.

Love of money as a disordered inner craving — the driving desire that corrupts the will. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected — filarguria is not about a relationship with money but about the inner craving. Correcting to Volition.
Loveliness as quality directing inner cognitive act of moral reflection. Automated label Moral Character corrected — Phili 4:8 is a directive to a cognitive act. Correcting to Cognition.
Love of good as inner character quality required of leaders. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected to Moral Character.
Love of brothers as relational inner disposition. Automated label confirmed.
Love of money as stable inner character disposition. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected to Moral Character.
Freedom from love of money as inner character quality — positive moral disposition of contentment. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected to Moral Character.

Love of pleasure as a disordered inner craving — the driving desire displacing God-orientation. Automated label Emotion-Positive corrected — filedonos names the craving/orientation not the experience. Correcting to Volition.

Love of God as the total inner orientation of the person. Automated label Cognition clearly misassigned — triggered by mind in the Shema-expansion. The commandment is the total-person relational orientation. Correcting to Relational Disposition.
Gods/Christs love as the preceding, enabling divine act. Automated label confirmed.
Love of neighbour and enemy as outward inner orientation. Automated label confirmed.
Mutual love as the inner relational orientation of the community. Automated label confirmed.
Disordered love as inner relational orientation toward wrong objects. Automated label confirmed.
Natural affection and friendship-love. Automated label confirmed. NONE — spans Christ (divine/human) and the world (human).

The ranking and ordering of inner loves — the volitional act of prioritising which love holds primacy. Automated label Relational Disposition corrected — the volitional ordering dimension is primary in both anchors. Correcting to Volition.
The natural warm inner affection of Peters love for Christ. Automated label confirmed.
The drive for primacy as stable inner character disposition. Automated label Cognition corrected to Moral Character.

record.description: Group 733-001 (ni.chum — compassionate comfort, Reg 23) is anchored by Hos 11:8: "my heart recoils within me; my compassion grows warm and tender." This verse names the divine inner life with visceral specificity — the heart recoiling, compassion warming. Session B for Reg 23 should examine whether this visceral inner-movement language for God represents a unique category within the compassion vocabulary: divine inner-being experience described through embodied sensation. The question for Session B is whether the compassion vocabulary of Reg 23 contains a sub-pattern of "visceral inner movement" terms (splanchnizō, ni.chum, ra.cha.mim) that share a somatic/embodied description of the inner compassion movement, and whether this sub-pattern spans the divine-human boundary (both God and humans described as "moved within" by compassion).

Luk 7:47 (anchor for group 5377-001) establishes an explicit inner-being grammar of forgiveness: the quantity of forgiveness received determines the intensity of the inner orientation of love produced — "she loved much because she was forgiven much; he who is forgiven little loves little." This is not merely a motivational claim but an inner-being causal chain: divine forgiveness (vertical release) → inner love-orientation (relational disposition). Session B for Reg 64 should examine whether this causal chain — forgiveness received → love orientation produced — is a recurring inner-being pattern across the forgiveness corpus, and how it relates to the grace-supplication chain already identified in Reg 68 (grace given → supplication produced, from DIM-068 Session C data). The hypothesis: forgiveness and grace both operate as inner-being generators that produce specific downstream inner orientations (love and supplication respectively).

Group 535-001 (ye.di.dut — beloved of Gods soul, Reg 103) is anchored by Jer 12:7: I have given the beloved of my soul into the hands of her enemies. The phrase ye.di.dut nap.shi (beloved of my soul/nephesh) attributes an intimate inner-being category to God — God has a nephesh, and the beloved relationship is located there. This raises a significant inner-being question for Session B of Reg 103: does the love vocabulary of Reg 103 contain a sub-pattern of terms that attribute inner-being states to God with the same vocabulary used for human inner-being states? The terms ye.di.dut (beloved of my soul), ra.cha.mim (my heart yearns — Jer 31:20 in group 551-001), and the dod vocabulary (God as lover/beloved in Song and prophets) may all encode a divine inner-being reality using the same vocabulary as the human inner person. Session B should investigate whether Reg 103 contains a theological claim about divine inner-being experience, and how this connects to the programmes governing question about the inner characteristics of the human person.

The Hebrew root CHASAD (Reg 23) crosses into Reg 146 (shame): H2616A cha.sad (be kind) and H2617B che.sed (steadfast love) are in Reg 23; H2616B cha.sad (to shame) is in Reg 146. The same root family spans the two poles of relational experience: covenantal love/mercy (chus, chesed) and shame/exposure. This is not incidental — the same etymological root covers both the relational bond at its most loyal and the relational wound at its most exposed. Session D should examine whether this root-family crossing reveals a structural relationship between mercy and shame in the inner-being vocabulary of Scripture: whether the depth of mercy presupposes the depth of shame, or whether the vocabulary itself encodes the relational restoration movement (from shame/exposure to mercy/covering). Relevant registries: C17 (compassion/love/kindness/mercy) and C06 or equivalent shame cluster.

Group 734-001 (sumpatheō — sympathy, Reg 23) is anchored by Heb 4:15: Christ's capacity to sympathize with human weaknesses, grounded in his own temptation. This is a case of genuine divine-human solidarity — the same inner capacity for fellow-feeling is attributed to the incarnate Son in his priestly role. Session D should examine whether the sympathy/compassion vocabulary of Reg 23 contains a sub-pattern of terms that describe the capacity for trans-boundary solidarity (God/human, Christ/human): splanchnizō (divine-human compassion in the Synoptics), oikteirō (sovereign divine compassion), sumpatheō (priestly solidarity through shared humanity), ra.cham (mother-womb metaphor). This sub-pattern may map onto a unique dimension of the C17 cluster: compassion as the inner quality that crosses boundaries of power, vulnerability, and status.

The covenant vocabulary of Reg 34 spans at least four distinct dimension categories across the be.rit and ka.rat groups: Relational Disposition (unconditional divine commitment, relational bond), Transformation (new covenant, heart-writing), Volition (bilateral obligation, obedience-commitment), and Moral Character (oath-keeping, integrity). This multi-dimensional structure is not a grouping failure — it reflects the genuine inner-being complexity of covenant as a category that encompasses relational bond, inner transformation, volitional commitment, and moral integrity simultaneously. Session D should examine whether the covenant vocabulary of Reg 34 serves as a structural backbone for the other C17 registries: whether love (Reg 103), mercy (Reg 111), compassion (Reg 23) and peace (Reg 117) can be understood as facets of the single inner-being architecture that covenant names. The hypothesis is that covenant in Reg 34 is not merely one registry among equals in C17 but the structural container within which the other relational-disposition registries operate.

Reg 62 (fellowship) has four groups that together trace three axes of koinōnia: (1) the divine-human correspondence axis (5367-001 — partaking of divine nature; 873-001 — communion with Father and Son), (2) the moral-character axis (5367-002 — solidarity/complicity), and (3) the horizontal-relational axis (873-002 — mutual belonging among believers). These three axes are not independent — the horizontal fellowship among believers (873-002) is explicitly grounded in the vertical fellowship with God (1Jo 1:3). Session D should examine whether the fellowship vocabulary of Reg 62 encodes a structural relationship: that genuine human inner-relational community (C17's relational-disposition cluster) is grounded in and derives from participation in the divine community (Father-Son-Spirit). This would make Reg 62 (fellowship) functionally analogous to Reg 34 (covenant) as a structural container: covenant names the bond, fellowship names the participatory inner reality of that bond.

Group 879-002 (afesis — the unforgivable sin, Mar 3:29) defines the absolute limit of forgiveness. Session D should examine whether the forgiveness vocabulary of Reg 64 encodes a structural polarity: at one end, God's unconditional new-covenant forgiveness that remembers sin no more (5379-003, Jer 31:34); at the other end, the permanent condition of unforgiven guilt (879-002, Mar 3:29). Between these poles, the registry maps the full spectrum of forgiveness experience — cultic atonement, divine mercy, human horizontal forgiveness, intercessory plea. This polarity — absolute forgiveness / absolute non-forgiveness — may be a unique structural feature of the forgiveness vocabulary compared to the other C17 relational registries (compassion, mercy, love) which do not have an analogous absolute limit.

The am-variant groups in Reg 99 (H5971H/I/L, each with three sub-groups) produce a large corpus (427 verses each for the -001 groups) where Moses's inner orientation of solidarity and advocacy appears as the primary inner-being content of the anchor verses. This raises a structural question: why are "people/am" terms placed in Reg 99 (kindness)? The intercessory solidarity of Moses (Num 14:19 — appealing to God's steadfast love on behalf of the people) may be the inner-being connection — Moses's advocacy for the people is itself an expression of kindness/solidarity as an inner orientation. Session D should examine whether the community-solidarity vocabulary of Reg 99 (the am-terms, the im.ma.di terms, the im-terms) encodes a dimension of kindness that is specifically communal and intercessory, rather than individual. The hypothesis: Reg 99 may contain a communal or social-solidarity sub-pattern that is distinct from the dyadic relational orientation of the love/compassion/mercy registries.

The dod groups in Reg 103 (533-001, 1602-001, 545-001) together span the full range of the beloved-love vocabulary: from the Songs human mutual delight to covenantal God-Israel love to intimate address. Session D should examine whether the dod/beloved vocabulary encodes a structural homology: the same inner-being quality of intense, exclusive, covenantal devotion manifests at both the divine-human covenantal level and the human interpersonal level. If confirmed across the full corpus, this would show that the love vocabulary of Scripture treats both divine and human love as expressions of the same inner-being characteristic — not mere analogy.

_patch_summary.note: No registry stamps — applied in Part 4
